mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies decline concurrently, or interaction term coefficient fails to exceed 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, real savings account balances act as a liquidity buffer for consumer spending; when RSAFS declines while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, equity demand weakens due to reduced disposable income flows.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain uncorrelated with RSAFS declines when rates exceed 3.5%, or cross-sectional momentum factors fully explain the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expansion directly increases corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 jobs signals an abrupt labor market cooling that precedes earnings downgrades, triggering negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward predictive accuracy drops below 50% when payroll declines exceed 100k, or signal fails to outperform a random baseline over 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises alongside rising unemployment, it indicates labor decoupling from output growth, which historically precedes margin compression and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew when both proxies move divergently, or interaction term coefficient fails to exceed 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, housing starts serve as a leading indicator of credit stress; a drop in HOUST below 950 units while FEDFUNDS rises accelerates equity sell-offs over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain uncorrelated with HOUST declines when rates rise rapidly, or cross-sectional value factors fully explain the signal.
